confirmations.reason: Project-Level AGENTS.md is a repository-scoped file that provides standing instructions and context to the agent, directly matching the capability description.
Plugins bundle skills, app integrations, and MCP servers into reusable workflows for Codex, directly matching the concept of bundled reusable capabilities the agent can load.

AGENTS.md provides persistent custom guidance/instructions, but the capability specifically targets shaping the system prompt, persona, or output style. AGENTS.md is more of a general instruction mechanism (closer to project-instruction-file), not specifically output or system-prompt customization.
Per-Agent Model Selection directly allows choosing which underlying model each agent runs on, matching the capability exactly.
MCP (Model Context Protocol) is described as the standard way to connect Codex to external tools and context providers, directly realizing the external tool/connector protocol capability.
MCP Server CLI Management provides a CLI command to add and manage MCP servers for Codex, directly realizing server management and configuration capability.
OAuth MCP Login explicitly authenticates to MCP servers via OAuth, directly matching the MCP authentication capability.

MCP Tool Exposure describes tools that MCP servers expose for the model to call, but it does not describe governance, allow-listing, or control over which tools the agent may use. It is about tool availability, not tool governance.

Skills are reusable instruction sets for specific task types, not durable memory that persists across turns/sessions based on past interactions. They are more akin to packaged instructions than persistent memory.
Container Caching caches configured containers to accelerate task startup, which is infrastructure-level caching, not prompt/context caching to reduce latency and cost of model calls.
Subagent Workflows describes Codex spawning specialized agents in parallel to handle parts of a task and consolidating results, directly matching the subagents/delegated agents capability.
Project Manager Multi-Agent Workflow describes a coordinator agent managing task hand-offs across a team of specialized agents, directly realizing multi-agent teams and messaging capability.

Automations run recurring tasks unattended in the background, which aligns more with scheduled/background execution than with defining repeatable, named multi-step task sequences (workflows/saved automations). The feature lacks the 'named multi-step sequence' aspect of the capability.
Git Worktree Isolation runs each automation in an isolated worktree to prevent interference, directly realizing parallel isolated workspaces capability.

Cloud Environments lets you control what Codex installs and runs during cloud tasks, with caching, secrets, and a default image, directly matching the managed cloud/container execution environment capability.
Standalone Automations are independent scheduled tasks that run on their own without a conversation thread, directly realizing background/async execution capability.
Thread Automations are recurring tasks triggered within a conversation thread, directly realizing scheduled/triggered runs without supervision.
The Sandbox is explicitly described as a constrained environment letting Codex run commands autonomously without unrestricted machine access, directly matching sandboxed execution capability.
Approval Policy controls when Codex must stop and ask before crossing sandbox boundaries, directly realizing tool permission controls (approve/deny what the agent may do).
Network Access Control enables or disables outbound network access with optional domain allow-listing, directly matching the network/egress control capability.
Codex Security is a security-review plugin and cloud service that scans repositories for vulnerabilities and validates findings, directly realizing security scanning/threat model capability.

Custom Setup Script runs a user-defined script for complex environment configuration, but this is not the same as running the agent inside a defined dev container. It lacks the dev container specification and isolation aspect of the capability.
Built-in Run Tracing emits structured records of model calls, tool calls, handoffs, and custom spans for each run, directly realizing audit logging/telemetry capability.
AI Code Review reviews GitHub PR diffs and posts standard GitHub code reviews focused on serious issues, directly matching the automated code review capability.
Evals API allows configuring and running evaluations of model outputs programmatically, directly realizing the evals/testing harness capability.
Multi-Device Session Handoff allows resuming an active Codex session from any signed-in device, directly realizing the resume/fork session capability (at least the resume aspect).
Remote Action Approval lets users review and approve Codex commands from a remote device, directly matching the remote control/approval capability.
Task Completion Notifications send alerts when Codex finishes a task or requires input, directly realizing the notifications capability.
Codex for Linear allows delegating work to Codex from Linear issues by assigning or mentioning @Codex, directly realizing chat-surface integration with issue/project tools.

GitHub Integration is one specific access surface (GitHub PRs and cloud tasks), not a feature that describes or enables the full range of access surfaces (terminal, IDE, web, cloud, mobile). It partially overlaps but does not directly realize the broad 'access surfaces' capability.
